title: Dense Peppery Egyptian Pancetta Casserole
description: A crumbly, peppery casserole that fuels post-workout recovery with pancetta and feta, infused with Egyptian spices.
instructions: In a skillet, heat 1 tbsp olive oil over medium heat and cook diced pancetta until crisp, about 5 minutes, then set aside.
In the same skillet, add remaining olive oil, then sauté diced onion until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in cumin, coriander, paprika, black pepper, and cayenne pepper, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Remove skillet from heat and whisk together eggs, feta cheese, and salt in a bowl until smooth.
Pour the egg mixture into the skillet, adding the cooked pancetta and parsley, stirring to combine.
Transfer the mixture into a greased casserole dish and top with chopped tomatoes.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the top is golden and the center is set.
Let the casserole rest for 5 minutes, then slice into squares.
Serve hot, optionally garnishing with extra parsley.
